sourceMovie.title: Mr. Nobody
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Mr. Nobody (2009) stuck because it commits to its science fiction premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Jaco Van Dormael's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The science fiction and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Her
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Her draws from the same science-fiction well as Mr. Nobody, channeling drama in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2013 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Vanilla Sky
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Vanilla Sky (2001) captures the science-fiction appeal of Mr. Nobody without simply imitating it. The romance streak running through both films is what puts Vanilla Sky at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: CODA
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The drama appeal of Mr. Nobody — that bittersweet edge, the romance — translates cleanly into CODA (2021). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: Primer
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Primer (2004) clearly understood what makes science-fiction work — the same quality that keeps Mr. Nobody rewatchable. Expect the same tense rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: Spring
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Spring (2014) is the kind of film Mr. Nobody fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The science-fiction overlap is real, but it's the dark emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: Last Year at Marienbad
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Last Year at Marienbad shares Mr. Nobody's gift for drama — the 1961 film delivers romance without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: Time Freak
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes Mr. Nobody compelling — the science-fiction, the romance — shows up in Time Freak (2018) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that funny storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: Frequencies
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The science-fiction energy of Mr. Nobody is alive in Frequencies (2013), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a tense payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: Veer-Zaara
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Veer-Zaara (2004) operates in the same drama as Mr. Nobody, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The bittersweet payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: Predestination
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Predestination (2014) belongs to the same conversation as Mr. Nobody — both anchor their science-fiction in thriller emotion that doesn't let go. The tense undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

faq.question: What should I watch after Mr. Nobody?
faq.answer: Try Her, Vanilla Sky, CODA—each shares DNA with Mr. Nobody while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Mr. Nobody?
faq.answer: Mr. Nobody (2009) is best described as Science Fiction, Drama, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Mr. Nobody worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this science fiction lane.
